thesis: every prime-distribution phenomenon factors as (all-finite-places closure product, decidable) x (zero-correlation interference, the single open wall)
scope: layer-1 results are classical (verified, not discovered); layer-2 is open in every conjectural row; nothing here proves RH, GRH, or any infinitude conjecture

rows.layer2_wall: infinitude <-> pair correlation of zeros (explicit formula); unconditional frontier: bounded gaps <= 246
rows.falsifier: Sieve further and find the count drifting from the HL integral; or exhibit a finite place at which the pattern {0,2} is inadmissible.

rows.phenomenon: prime pairs at every even gap (Polignac)
rows.layer2_wall: infinitude for any fixed h open; same zero-pair-correlation wall as row 1
rows.gate: every gap ratio within 2% of its all-places local product
rows.falsifier: Find an even gap whose abundance ratio is NOT the product of (p-1)/(p-2) over odd primes dividing it.

rows.layer1_classical_source: THEOREM: Dirichlet (1837); PNT in APs (de la Vallee Poussin)
rows.layer2_wall: error term: square-root cancellation = GRH for Dirichlet L-functions
rows.gate: inadmissible classes empty above q; admissible classes within 1% of equidistribution
rows.falsifier: Find a prime > q in a residue class not coprime to q, or persistent skew between admissible classes (beyond Chebyshev-bias scale, row 5).

rows.phenomenon: splitting of primes in Q(sqrt 5) (the programme's base field)
rows.layer2_wall: error term = zeros of zeta_K / L(s, chi_5)

computed.note: Brandt prime ideals in provenance_664.json lie over these primes

rows.gate: split fraction within 1% of 1/2; exactly one twin pair with p = 3 mod 5 (namely (3,5))
rows.falsifier: Find a prime p = +-2 mod 5 that splits (or +-1 that does not), or a second twin pair with p = 3 mod 5.
